title: 47-year-old man with central chest pain
candidateInstructions: You are a Foundation Year 2 doctor working in the Emergency Department. Mr David Thompson, a 47-year-old electrician, has been brought in by ambulance with central chest pain that started approximately 2 hours ago. His vital signs on arrival: BP 152/94 mmHg, HR 102 bpm, RR 20, SpO2 96% on air, Temp 36.8°C. An ECG has been performed and is available on request. Please take a focused history, request appropriate investigations, and explain your initial management plan to the patient. You do NOT need to perform a physical examination.
examinerNotes: Story consistent with acute inferior STEMI. Candidate should: take SOCRATES pain history, elicit cardiovascular risk factors, request ECG and troponin promptly, recognise STEMI, initiate aspirin 300mg + ticagrelor 180mg + UFH, arrange primary PCI within 120 minutes.

patient.name: David Thompson
patient.presentingComplaint: Central crushing chest pain for 2 hours
patient.reasonForAttendance: I've got really bad chest pain, doctor

hpi.site: Central chest, retrosternal — patient places clenched fist on sternum
hpi.onset: Sudden, while sitting watching TV after dinner
hpi.character: Crushing, like a heavy weight on my chest
hpi.radiation: Down the inside of my left arm, and into my jaw on the left side
hpi.timing: Started 2 hours ago, constant — not coming and going
hpi.exacerbatingFactors: Worse when I tried to walk
hpi.severity: 9 out of 10 — worst pain I've ever had
hpi.previousEpisodes: Had some chest tightness on exertion last month but it went away

socialHistory.diet: Mostly takeaway, lots of red meat

systemsReview.cardiovascular: Some ankle swelling at end of long days

examination.general: Diaphoretic, anxious-looking man, clutching chest, in obvious distress
examination.cardiovascular: Heart sounds I and II normal, no murmurs, JVP not elevated
examination.respiratory: Equal bilateral air entry, vesicular breath sounds

investigations.name: 12-lead Electrocardiogram
investigations.result: Sinus rhythm at 102 bpm. ST elevation 2.5mm in lead II, 3mm in lead III, 2mm in lead aVF. Reciprocal ST depression 1.5mm in leads I and aVL. Q waves developing in lead III. Corrected QT 420ms.
investigations.interpretation: ACUTE INFERIOR ST-ELEVATION MYOCARDIAL INFARCTION. Territory: inferior, supplied by right coronary artery. Reciprocal changes confirm diagnosis.

investigations.name: High-sensitivity Troponin I

result.text: Significantly elevated. Repeat at 3 hours recommended.

investigations.interpretation: Markedly elevated troponin consistent with acute myocardial necrosis.

investigations.name: Urea & Electrolytes
investigations.interpretation: Normal renal function. Important for contrast safety for PCI.

investigations.name: Erect Chest X-ray (PA)
investigations.result: Cardiac silhouette normal size. Lungs clear. No pneumothorax. No widened mediastinum. No effusion.
investigations.interpretation: Normal chest x-ray — excludes aortic dissection and pneumothorax.

patient.emotionalState: Anxious and frightened. Worried this is 'the big one' like his father. May ask 'am I going to die, doctor?'
patient.communicationStyle: Speaks in short sentences due to pain and anxiety. Cooperative. Uses lay language only.
hiddenConcerns: I'm scared this is what killed my dad
Worried about my wife and kids
Don't want to lose my business — I'm self-employed
doNotVolunteer: Severity score — only give 9/10 if asked on a scale of 0 to 10
Family history — only mention if asked about family history
Smoking history — only mention if asked about smoking
Previous chest tightness — only if asked 'have you had this before?'
Hypertension — only if asked about medical conditions or medications
ECG findings — you are a layperson and do not know

dataGathering.detailedExpectation: Asks about Site, Onset, Character, Radiation, Associated symptoms, Timing, Exacerbating/relieving factors, Severity
Smoking, hypertension, diabetes, hypercholesterolaemia, family history of CVD

dataGathering.criterion: Takes a drug history including allergies
dataGathering.detailedExpectation: Current medications, doses, compliance, any allergies

clinicalManagement.detailedExpectation: States diagnosis of STEMI/inferior MI after viewing ECG
Aspirin 300mg AND second antiplatelet (ticagrelor 180mg or clopidogrel 600mg)
GTN if BP allows; morphine for pain
Recognises need for primary PCI within 120 minutes

interpersonalSkills.criterion: Introduces self with name and role
interpersonalSkills.detailedExpectation: Gives name, role, confirms patient identity
Uses 'heart attack' or 'blocked artery', avoids jargon

interpersonalSkills.criterion: Explains treatment plan and seeks consent

interpersonalSkills.detailedExpectation: Asks about or responds to patient's worries

learningObjectives: Recognise the typical clinical presentation of STEMI
Take a focused cardiovascular history using SOCRATES
Identify ST elevation on a 12-lead ECG and localise the territory
Initiate immediate STEMI management per NICE NG185
Activate primary PCI pathway within target times
